reason: Close the ranks (#470) contracts a unit's auto frontage one notch once casualties cross a 50% threshold, so a mauled regiment reforms narrower and deeper instead of holding a thin, wide line. Staging this in a live recorded battle turned out to reliably misrepresent it: at any casualty ratio steep enough to grind a defender below 50% strength in a short clip, the enemy's much larger mass visually envelops and occludes the defending unit's own block by the time the contraction fires, so the clip would show a red blob swallowing a hidden unit rather than the frontage change itself. Verified instead with a state-dump (soldiers/frontage fields agree with UnitFormation.should_close_ranks at every sampled tick) and a before/after screenshot pair committed to demos/shots/ (close-ranks-full.png: 30 soldiers, full 8-file width; close-ranks-contracted.png: 14 soldiers after the threshold crossing, narrowed to 4 files and visibly deeper) and embedded in the PR description. Covered by test/unit/test_unit_formation.gd (the pure should_close_ranks/frontage predicates) and test/unit/test_unit.gd (the per-tick contract/recover/hysteresis behavior).
